reasoning: The FRED data shows expected inflation bounded tightly between 2.10% and 2.49% over the past year, currently sitting at 2.305% in August 2025. It acts largely as a mean-reverting series or random walk in the short term. The combined statistical estimate points slightly toward an increase over the next month (P=0.52) due to a modest upward linear drift overcoming negative seasonality. Since movements in highly tracked macroeconomic expectation series are extremely difficult to predict and approximate a random walk, the probabilities will be kept very close to 50%. As the forecast horizon extends to November 2025 and February 2026, uncertainty increases and the probabilities gradually revert to the uninformative prior of 0.50.
